title: dysphania bhutanica
intro: Dysphania bhutanica, also known as Chenopodium bhutanica, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family, native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. Dysphania bhutanica has been reported to exhibit several health benefits, including the treatment of worm infestations, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential role in managing diabetes and hypertension. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
